title: Ch 9.3 — Custom Exceptions: Evil Loan Approval
description:
Practice creating and throwing a custom checked exception.

──────────────────────────────────────────────
Background
──────────────────────────────────────────────

The Bank of Evil needs a loan approval system. Loan applications
can fail for several reasons, and we want a single custom exception
type — LoanException — to describe every failure with a clear message.

──────────────────────────────────────────────
Step 1 — Create LoanException (checked exception)
──────────────────────────────────────────────

  class LoanException extends Exception {
      public LoanException(String message) {
          super(message);
      }
  }

  KEY POINT: It extends Exception (not RuntimeException),
  so the compiler REQUIRES callers to catch or declare it.

──────────────────────────────────────────────
Step 2 — Complete LoanApprovalSystem
──────────────────────────────────────────────

  Field:
    • double availableFunds  (set by constructor)

  Method 1: LoanRequest validateLoan(String villainName,
                                     String amountStr,
                                     String scoreStr)
            throws LoanException

    • Parse amountStr → double. If parsing fails, throw:
        new LoanException("Invalid amount: " + amountStr)

    • Parse scoreStr → int. If parsing fails, throw:
        new LoanException("Invalid score: " + scoreStr)

    • If villainName is null or blank, throw:
        new LoanException("Villain name required")

    • If amount < 1000 or amount > 5_000_000, throw:
        new LoanException("Amount out of range")

    • If score < 1 or score > 10, throw:
        new LoanException("Score must be 1-10")

    • If all valid, return new LoanRequest(villainName, amount, score)

  Method 2: void approveLoan(LoanRequest req) throws LoanException

    • If req.evilnessScore < 7, throw:
        new LoanException("Insufficient evilness")

    • If availableFunds < req.amount, throw:
        new LoanException("Insufficient funds")

    • Otherwise subtract req.amount from availableFunds.

  Method 3: String processApplication(String name,
                                      String amountStr,
                                      String scoreStr)

    Use try-catch-finally:
    • TRY: call validateLoan(), then approveLoan().
      If both succeed, set result = "Approved: " + name
    • CATCH LoanException: set result = "Denied: " + e.getMessage()
    • FINALLY: result += " (processed)"
    • Return result.

──────────────────────────────────────────────
LoanRequest class (already provided — do not change)
──────────────────────────────────────────────

  Fields (package-visible): String villainName; double amount; int evilnessScore;
  Constructor: LoanRequest(String villainName, double amount, int evilnessScore)

──────────────────────────────────────────────
HINTS
──────────────────────────────────────────────
• Use Double.parseDouble() for amount, Integer.parseInt() for score.
• Wrap each parse in try-catch for NumberFormatException, then
  throw LoanException from inside the catch block.
• String.isBlank() or .trim().isEmpty() checks for blank names.
• Declare String result = ""; before the try so finally can see it.
